description: Sync messages from Telegram into your vault through a self-hosted Supabase backend. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.name: Telegram Bridge

name: Telegram Bridge

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 0.1.1

### Fixed
- **Path templates no longer create stray sub-folders.** Slashes inside a variable value (for example a URL in `{{content}}`, or a `/` in a chat/topic name) are now sanitized to `_` in path mode, while the literal separators in your template are preserved. A message containing `https://radio.garden/x` now lands in a single note instead of nested folders.

### Internal / community-review compliance
- Fixed the build so it passes on current TypeScript (removed deprecated `baseUrl` and `moduleResolution: node10` tsconfig options).
- Replaced the `builtin-modules` build dependency with Node's built-in `node:module`.
- Use Obsidian's `createDiv()` / `createSpan()` helpers instead of `createEl("div" | "span")`.
- Reference `crypto.randomUUID()` directly instead of `globalThis` (popout-window safe).
- Removed `!important` from styles; the same overrides are now done via selector specificity.
- Added proper types to Supabase responses and settings loading, eliminating unsafe `any` usage.

No configuration changes are required to upgrade.
